data.content:
This is an **Android tombstone** crash log from a .NET MAUI app using the Mono runtime (`libmonosgen-2.0.so`). Let me identify the frames and attempt to download debug symbols for symbolication.

**Key details:**
- **Signal**: SIGABRT ("not compatible")
- **Runtime frames**: #01–#07 in `libmonosgen-2.0.so`
- **BuildId**: `1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be`
- **Frame #08**: JIT-compiled managed code (anonymous memory)

Let me try to fetch debug symbols from Microsoft's symbol server.

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.command:
cd /tmp/sv-611a5aedab034b7faa8c087fed3a9fd2 && \
BUILDID="1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be" && \
echo "Trying Microsoft symbol server URLs..." && \
echo "--- Attempt 1: elf-buildid-sym ---" && \
curl -sSL -w "HTTP %{http_code}\n" -o /dev/null "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libmonosgen-2.0.so/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/libmonosgen-2.0.so" && \
echo "--- Attempt 2: elf-buildid (no sym) ---" && \
curl -sSL -w "HTTP %{http_code}\n" -o /dev/null "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libmonosgen-2.0.so/elf-buildid-${BUILDID}/libmonosgen-2.0.so" && \
echo "--- Attempt 3: _.debug format ---" && \
curl -sSL -w "HTTP %{http_code}\n" -o /dev/null "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/_.debug/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/_.debug"
arguments.description: Try downloading symbols from Microsoft symbol server

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Try downloading symbols from Microsoft symbol server

data.reasoningText:
This is an Android crash log (tombstone format), not an Apple .ips crash log. The apple-crash-symbolication skill is specifically for Apple platform crash logs (.ips files), so it won't be applicable here.

Let me analyze this crash log:

1. **Platform**: Android (arm64)
2. **App**: `com.companyname.MauiTest` - a .NET MAUI app
3. **Signal**: SIGABRT with abort message "not compatible"
4. **Runtime**: Mono (`libmonosgen-2.0.so`) - this is the .NET runtime used on Android for MAUI apps


5. **Build ID**: `1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be`

The crash stack shows the .NET runtime frames (#01-#07) all within `libmonosgen-2.0.so`, with frame #08 pointing to anonymous memory that's likely JIT-compiled managed code, and frame #00 being the actual abort call in libc. To properly symbolicate these frames, I'd need the debug symbols for that specific build of the Mono runtime.

Microsoft publishes symbols for their ELF binaries on their symbol server, so I can try constructing the right URL to fetch the debug symbols using the BuildId and attempting to download them from either the Microsoft symbol server or a debuginfod server. I'm trying different symbol server URLs to locate the debug information, and I could also use the `dotnet-symbol` tool as an alternative approach to download what I need.

arguments.command:
cd /tmp/sv-611a5aedab034b7faa8c087fed3a9fd2 && \
BUILDID="1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be" && \
echo "Trying Microsoft symbol server URLs..." && \
echo "--- Attempt 1: elf-buildid-sym ---" && \
curl -sSL -w "HTTP %{http_code}\n" -o /dev/null "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libmonosgen-2.0.so/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/libmonosgen-2.0.so" && \
echo "--- Attempt 2: elf-buildid (no sym) ---" && \
curl -sSL -w "HTTP %{http_code}\n" -o /dev/null "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libmonosgen-2.0.so/elf-buildid-${BUILDID}/libmonosgen-2.0.so" && \
echo "--- Attempt 3: _.debug format ---" && \
curl -sSL -w "HTTP %{http_code}\n" -o /dev/null "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/_.debug/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/_.debug"
arguments.description: Try downloading symbols from Microsoft symbol server

Trying Microsoft symbol server URLs...
